content:
## Duplicate Charge Intake
1. Treat a claimed repeated debit-card or electronic-fund-transfer posting as a duplicate charge only when the customer's issue is the repeated posting itself, not card loss, theft, or fraud.
2. For duplicate charge disputes, the card action is keep_active. Do not replace or close the card only because this category was selected.
3. Record the dispute category before discussing liability or provisional credit, because unauthorized categories follow a different customer-liability path. See "Internal: Debit Card Dispute Intake Requirements" for intake requirements.

## Liability Treatment
- The liability rule applies to unauthorized_transaction, card_present_fraud, card_not_present_fraud.
- If one of those categories is reported within 2 business days after discovery, maximum liability is 50.
- If it is reported after that period but within the statement window, maximum liability is 500.
- If it is reported after the statement window, apply unlimited liability in this benchmark policy.
- For a duplicate charge that is not an unauthorized error, customer liability is 0. See "Debit Card Error Notice And Liability" for the full liability schedule.

## Provisional Credit Review
Duplicate charge is included in the categories that allow provisional credit: unauthorized_transaction, card_present_fraud, card_not_present_fraud, atm_cash_discrepancy, duplicate_charge. Confirm the category remains duplicate_charge before using that rule; if the customer changes the claim to unauthorized use or fraud, reclassify before continuing. See "Debit Card Provisional Credit Policy" for credit timing and handling details.
title: Internal: Duplicate Charge Handling
